items.final_wording: Supports urgent cardiovascular assessment for ECG abnormality, heart failure, transient loss of consciousness during exertion, family history of sudden cardiac death, new or unexplained breathlessness, or a heart murmur.

source.title: Transient loss of consciousness ('blackouts') in over 16s
source.population: Adults and young people aged 16 years and older with suspected transient loss of consciousness.
source.setting: Initial assessment, emergency transfer, referral and specialist evaluation of transient loss of consciousness.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected event-history, witness-account, vital-sign, cardiovascular or neurological assessment, ECG-result, urgent-risk and transfer-of-record documentation. It is international guidance and does not establish the diagnosis or UAE disposition.

source.title: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
source.population: Adults receiving cardiovascular risk assessment or lipid-modification review for primary or secondary prevention.
source.setting: Primary care and other clinician-led cardiovascular prevention services.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult cardiovascular risk and lipid-modification assessment and monitoring; international only and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.3.12 — cardiovascular risk and lifestyle review

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-led cardiovascular risk review and documented lifestyle discussion without autonomously generating advice.

items.rationale: The NICE section supports clinician-led cardiovascular-risk assessment that includes family-history context, other risk factors, patient preferences, and documented discussion without calculating a score or diagnosing an inherited condition.

items.rationale: The TLoC section supports documenting a family history of sudden cardiac death as an assessed red flag only when transient loss of consciousness is relevant; it does not infer inherited risk or urgency in every family-history review.
